---
title: "Kmeans_preProcessing_4"
author: "Joey Frost-Xenopoulos - 0730136"
date: "2025-01-31"
output: html_document
---

```{r, warning = FALSE}
# Setting up the matrices for processing
library(magick)
library(raster)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(dplyr)
set.seed(123)

# Define directories and the base name (without extension)
server_dir <- normalizePath(
  Sys.getenv("MAGNETO_SERVER_DIR", unset = "D:/SERVER/1902"),
  winslash = "/",
  mustWork = FALSE
)
base_name <- "AGC-D-19020110-19020112"

# Construct paths using the base name
tif_path <- file.path(server_dir, paste0(base_name, ".tif"))
rds_path <- file.path(server_dir, paste0(base_name, ".tif-Digitized.rds"))

# Load and process the image
img <- image_read(tif_path)
if (image_info(img)$width < image_info(img)$height) {
  img <- image_rotate(img, -90)
}

# Convert the .tif file to a matrix
mymat <- as(raster(tif_path), "matrix")

# Load the .rds file
img_rds <- readRDS(rds_path)

# Reshape the matrix for clustering
mymat_long <- melt(mymat)
colnames(mymat_long) <- c("X", "Y", "Value")

# Remove NAs (if any) and normalize the values
mymat_long <- na.omit(mymat_long)
mymat_long$Value <- scale(mymat_long$Value)

# Assuming mymat is your large raster matrix
subset_mymat <- mymat[,500:1100]

# Reshape the matrix for clustering
mymat_long <- melt(subset_mymat)
colnames(mymat_long) <- c("X", "Y", "Value")

# Remove NAs (if any) and normalize the values
mymat_long <- na.omit(mymat_long)
mymat_long$Value <- scale(mymat_long$Value)

kmeans_result <- kmeans(mymat_long$Value, centers = 3)  # Adjust centers as needed
mymat_long$Cluster <- as.factor(kmeans_result$cluster)

# # Plot the clustered matrix
# ggplot(mymat_long, aes(x = X, y = Y, fill = Cluster)) +
#   geom_tile() +
#   theme_minimal() +
#   coord_fixed() +
#   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +
#   labs(title = "Clustered Matrix", x = "X Coordinate", y = "Y Coordinate")

# Calculate the number of data points in each cluster
cluster_counts <- mymat_long %>%
  group_by(Cluster) %>%
  summarize(count = n())

# Sort clusters by the count
cluster_counts <- cluster_counts %>% arrange(desc(count))

# Remove the first observation (biggest one)
viable_clusters <- cluster_counts[-1, ]

# Filter mymat_long for rows where Cluster is in viable_clusters$Cluster
filtered_data_0 <- mymat_long %>%
  filter(Cluster %in% viable_clusters$Cluster)

clusters <- unique(filtered_data_0$Cluster)
# Subset into filtered_data_1 and filtered_data_2
filtered_data_1 <- filtered_data_0 %>%
  filter(Cluster == clusters[1])
filtered_data_2 <- filtered_data_0 %>%
  filter(Cluster == clusters[2])

# Find mean of each distribution
var_Y_1 <- round(var(filtered_data_1$Y), 0)
# exact_mean_rows_1 <- filtered_data_1 %>%
#   filter(Y == mean_Y_1)
var_Y_2 <- round(var(filtered_data_2$Y), 0)
# exact_mean_rows_2 <- filtered_data_2 %>%
#   filter(Y == mean_Y_2)

if(var_Y_1 < var_Y_2) {
  cluster_2 <- filtered_data_1
  cluster_3 <- filtered_data_2
} else {
  cluster_2 <-  filtered_data_2
  cluster_3 <-  filtered_data_1
}

plot(cluster_2$X, cluster_2$Y)
abline(h = mean(cluster_2$Y), col = "red")
plot(cluster_3$X, cluster_3$Y)
abline(h = mean(cluster_3$Y), col = "red")
```


```{r}
library(mclust)
library(tclust)
library(dbscan)
dat <- data.frame(X = cluster_2$X, Y = cluster_2$Y)
# Applying Mclust to seperate the two lines
# G = 2 is the ideal two mixture components 
obj <- Mclust(dat, G = 2)
# Plotting the classification
plot(cluster_2$X, cluster_2$Y, col = obj$classification)


mac <- dbscan(dat, eps = 8, minPts = 1)
plot(cluster_2$X, cluster_2$Y, col = mac$cluster)
```

```{r}
# Count points per cluster (excluding noise, cluster 0)
cluster_counts <- table(mac$cluster)
cluster_counts <- cluster_counts[names(cluster_counts) != "0"]  # Ignore noise if present

# Get the two largest clusters
top_clusters <- as.numeric(names(sort(cluster_counts, decreasing = TRUE)[1:2]))

# Filter `dat` to retain only these two clusters
filtered_dat <- dat[mac$cluster %in% top_clusters, ]

# Add the cluster labels to the filtered data
filtered_dat$cluster <- mac$cluster[mac$cluster %in% top_clusters]

# Compute mean Y for each group
mean_group1 <- mean(subset(filtered_dat, cluster == top_clusters[1])$Y)
mean_group2 <- mean(subset(filtered_dat, cluster == top_clusters[2])$Y)

# Assign labels based on mean values
if (mean_group1 > mean_group2) {
  top_trace <- subset(filtered_dat, cluster == top_clusters[1])
  bot_trace <- subset(filtered_dat, cluster == top_clusters[2])
} else {
  top_trace <- subset(filtered_dat, cluster == top_clusters[1])
  bot_trace <- subset(filtered_dat, cluster == top_clusters[2])
}

# Compute median Y values for each unique X
median_Y_top <- top_trace %>%
  group_by(X) %>%
  summarise(median_Y = median(Y))

median_Y_bot <- bot_trace %>%
  group_by(X) %>%
  summarise(median_Y = median(Y))

plot(img)
lines(median_Y_bot$X, median_Y_bot$median_Y + 520, col = "red", lwd = 2)
lines(median_Y_top$X, median_Y_top$median_Y + 520, col = "red", lwd = 2)



```


```{r}
# Extract cluster classifications
dat$cluster_label <- obj$classification

# Compute mean Y for each group
mean_group1 <- mean(subset(cluster_2, dat$cluster_label == 1)$Y)
mean_group2 <- mean(subset(cluster_2, dat$cluster_label == 2)$Y)

# Assign labels based on mean values
if (mean_group1 > mean_group2) {
  top_trace <- subset(cluster_2, dat$cluster_label == 1)
  bot_trace <- subset(cluster_2, dat$cluster_label == 2)
} else {
  top_trace <- subset(cluster_2, dat$cluster_label == 2)
  bot_trace <- subset(cluster_2, dat$cluster_label == 1)
}

# Define the start and end X values
start_x <- img_rds$TopTraceStartEnds$Start + 125
end_x <- img_rds$TopTraceStartEnds$End + 125
top_trace <- subset(top_trace, X >= start_x & X <= end_x)

# Define the start and end X values
start_x <- img_rds$BottomTraceStartEnds$Start + 125
end_x <- img_rds$BottomTraceStartEnds$End + 125
bot_trace <- subset(bot_trace, X >= start_x & X <= end_x)

# Compute median Y values for each unique X
median_Y_top <- top_trace %>%
  group_by(X) %>%
  summarise(median_Y = median(Y))
plot(top_trace$X, top_trace$Y, ylim = c(0, 1000))
lines(median_Y_top$X, median_Y_top$median_Y, col = "red", lwd = 2)

median_Y_bot <- bot_trace %>%
  group_by(X) %>%
  summarise(median_Y = median(Y))
plot(bot_trace$X, bot_trace$Y, ylim = c(0, 1000))
lines(median_Y_bot$X, median_Y_bot$median_Y, col = "red", lwd = 2)

plot(img)
lines(median_Y_bot$X, median_Y_bot$median_Y + 600, col = "red", lwd = 2)
lines(median_Y_top$X, median_Y_top$median_Y + 600, col = "red", lwd = 2)

```
